free_text: Refractive scattering and the nuclear rainbow in the
interaction of C-12, C-13 with C-12 at 20 MeV/N.
The heavy ion accelerator VICKSI.
C-12 targets of 100-500 mug/cm**2 thickness have been
used.
Absolute cross sections were measured with a double
target consisting of 591.5 mug/cm**2 carbon and 181.5
mug/cm**2 gold.
Q3D magnetic spectrograph.
A gas filled proportional wire chamber with two
resistive wires for the measurements of two positions
and energy losses.
A position sensitive parallel plate avalanche
counter is used at the entrance of the Q3D-spectrometer
to measure the scattering angles of each particle
trajectory. The PPAC has been operated with 4 foils of
60 mug/cm**2 thickness and a gas pressure of 16 mbar
of isobutane.
A NE102A scintillator in the back for energy and
time-of-flight measurements.
*EN-RSL*.The energy resolution in the focal plane was
300 keV.
.Coupled-channel and optical model calculations.
C-13 spectrum of the elastic and inelastic scattering
on C-12 at 20 MeV/N have been reported.
*By authors*.The absolute normalization of the cross
section was achieved with the double target method. The
elastic scattering has been measured on a target
consisting of foils of C-12 with 591.5 mug/cm**2 and
Au-197 with 181.5 mug/cm**2 between 3.5 and 6.5 degrees
.The error is reported by authors on fig.
.Digitizing error of data.
.Digitizing error of angle.
.By Cajad.
